class.name: GDLlama
class.brief_description: A node that acts as a bridge to the llama.cpp library for AI text generation tasks. Docs: https://github.com/xarillian/GDLlama/tree/master/docs

method.name: load_model
method.description: Loads the model specified by the [member model_path] property into memory. Returns [code]@GlobalScope.OK[/code] on success.

method.name: unload_model
method.description: Unloads the currently loaded model from memory, freeing resources.

method.name: is_model_loaded
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if a model is currently loaded and ready for use.

method.name: generate_text_async
method.description: Starts an asynchronous (non-blocking) generation. The result is delivered via the [signal generate_text_finished] signal.

method.name: generate_chat
method.description: A synchronous (blocking) method that maintains conversational context between calls.

method.name: generate_chat_async
method.description: The asynchronous (non-blocking) version of generate_chat. Delivers the result via a signal.

method.name: stop_generate_text
method.description: Sends a stop signal to the currently running asynchronous generation.

method.name: is_running
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if an asynchronous generation thread is currently active.

method.name: reset_context
method.description: Clears the model's conversational memory (the KV cache).

method.name: compute_embedding
method.description: Generates a vector embedding for the given prompt synchronously (blocking).

method.name: compute_embedding_async
method.description: Starts an asynchronous (non-blocking) embedding generation. The result is delivered via the [signal embedding_computed] or [signal embedding_failed] signal.

method.name: similarity_cos
method.description: A utility function that calculates the cosine similarity between two vector embeddings.

members.member: The file path to your llama-compatible model file (e.g., [code]"res://models/model.gguf"[/code]).
The seed for the random number generator. -1 means a random seed will be used.
The maximum number of new tokens the model should generate in a single run.
Controls randomness. Lower values make the output more deterministic.
Reduces the pool of tokens to the 'k' most likely ones.
Nucleus sampling. Considers the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds 'p'.
If [code]true[/code], the model will not stop when it generates an End-of-Sequence token.
Penalizes the model for repeating tokens it has recently used (1.0 = no penalty).
The number of recent tokens to consider for the repetition penalty.
A custom chat template in Jinja2 format. If empty, the model's built-in template is used.
The number of model layers to offload to the GPU for acceleration. -1 uses the default recommended value.
The context window size in tokens.
The number of tokens to process in parallel during prompt evaluation.
The index of the primary GPU to use for miscellaneous computations.

signal.name: generate_text_updated
signal.description: Emitted repeatedly during an async generation, providing new tokens as they are generated.

signal.name: generate_text_finished
signal.description: Emitted once when an async generation has completed.

signal.name: generate_text_error
signal.description: Emitted once when async generation has completed with an error.

signal.name: embedding_computed
signal.description: Emitted when an async embedding generation is successful.

signal.name: embedding_failed
signal.description: Emitted if an error occurs during async embedding generation.
